examples.title: Algorithm: First-Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Allocates the first available memory block that is large enough for the process
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FirstFitAllocation
BEGIN
 FOR each block in memory_blocks DO
 IF block is free AND block.size >= process_size THEN
 Allocate process to this block
 Split block into allocated and remaining free part (if needed)
 RETURN allocation successful
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN allocation failed (no suitable block found)
END
examples.explanation:
1. Iterates through all memory blocks
2. Checks for first block that can accommodate the process
3. Allocates and splits block if necessary
4. Returns success immediately upon finding suitable block

examples.title: Algorithm: Best-Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Finds the smallest available memory block that satisfies the process requirement
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BestFitAllocation
BEGIN
 Initialize best_block = NULL
 Initialize min_size = INFINITY
 FOR each block in memory_blocks DO
 IF block is free AND block.size >= process_size THEN
 IF block.size < min_size THEN
 best_block = block
 min_size = block.size
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF best_block != NULL THEN
 Allocate process to best_block
 Split block if needed
 RETURN success
 ELSE
 RETURN failure
 END IF
END
examples.explanation:
1. Maintains track of smallest suitable block
2. Compares all blocks that meet size requirement
3. Selects block with minimum internal fragmentation
4. Allocates after full scan of memory blocks

examples.title: Algorithm: Worst-Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Selects the largest available memory block to minimize leftover small fragments
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WorstFitAllocation
BEGIN
 Initialize worst_block = NULL
 Initialize max_size = -INFINITY
 FOR each block in memory_blocks DO
 IF block is free AND block.size >= process_size THEN
 IF block.size > max_size THEN
 worst_block = block
 max_size = block.size
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF worst_block != NULL THEN
 Allocate process to worst_block
 Split block if needed
 RETURN success
 ELSE
 RETURN failure
 END IF
END
examples.explanation:
1. Tracks largest available block
2. Maximizes remaining space after allocation
3. Requires full scan of memory blocks
4. Helps reduce small fragment creation
